name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under iconic brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. The company is executing its 'Breaking New Ground' strategy, combining AI, autonomy, and precision technology with legacy heavy equipment. Q1 2026 consolidated revenue was $3.83B (flat YoY) with net income of $10M as the agricultural equipment downcycle continues. Plans to double Precision Tech sales to 10% of agriculture revenue by 2030.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): CNH has consistently introduced precision agriculture technology with over 15 new tractor launches, 10 harvesting products, and 30+ precision tech releases planned through 2027 [1]. The 2022 Iveco Group spinoff was a transformative structural simplification creating a pure-play ag/construction company. AI Deployment (2/2): CNH's 2025 Tech Day showcased AI-powered solutions shipping in production: autonomous combines and tractors making real-time decisions, the 'AI Tech Assistant' deployed to hundreds of dealer groups for diagnostic support, and every global leadership team member running generative or agentic AI pilot programs [3]. CNH took a minority investment in drone imaging AI company Bem Agro [3]. Market Share (1/2): CNH holds category leadership in agricultural equipment alongside Deere and AGCO globally with strong brand equity through Case IH and New Holland, though losing share in the current downcycle [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Precision Tech target of doubling to 10% of agriculture revenue by 2030 provides specific commercial milestones, with 30+ new releases through 2027 [1][3]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is slightly below industry leader Deere as a percentage of revenue.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CNH's purpose of 'Breaking New Ground' connects equipment manufacturing to sustainable food production and global food security — CEO Gerrit Marx has framed AI in agriculture as a 'generational shift' enabling farmers to make bigger decisions better [1][3]. This is specific and thoughtful, with genuine intentionality connecting the company's work to feeding a growing world sustainably. Community Impact (0/2): Employee foundations exist but no distinctive charitable programs with specific dollar amounts or measurable community impact were identified. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor reviews show mixed sentiment with 3.3/5 culture rating; criticisms include poor management, slow innovation, and siloed departments, though 'AI ML Research is cutting edge' was noted positively [3]. Recent leadership reorganization under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024) adds transitional uncertainty. Insider Buying (1/2): No meaningful net insider buying identified in the last 12 months; insider transactions are minimal or roughly balanced.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as a Growth stock on available metrics. Forward P/E of 14.4 is well below the 20 target (score 1.0), and forward PEG of 0.6 is excellent at below the 1.0 target (score 1.0, 2x weight), suggesting the market prices meaningful earnings recovery [1][4]. However, D/E of 333.7% exceeds the 300% floor (score 0.0) — elevated leverage typical for equipment companies with financial services arms. ROE of 4.8% is weak (score 0.32, 2x weight), profit margin of 2.1% is thin reflecting the cyclical trough (score 0.11), and revenue growth 5yr CAGR of approximately -8% is below the -5% floor (score 0.0, 2x weight) [4]. Revenue YoY of -0.1% in Q1 2026 shows stabilization from the -9% FY2025 decline (score 0.66). Weighted sum: 4.42/10 = 4.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9 — PROTECTED): CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment — tractors, harvesters, construction machinery — that cannot be replaced by AI software [1]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products with significant aftermarket services, dealer networks, and financing arms, all protected from AI substitution. Regulatory requirements for farm equipment safety, physical-world operations, and dealer-based distribution create structural moats. However, competitors like John Deere are investing heavily in autonomous and AI-powered equipment that could outpace CNH's technology roadmap, and CNH faces acknowledged cybersecurity risks and 'AI constraints' that could limit innovation [3]. AI Opportunity (1/1): CNH's AI Tech Assistant is deployed across hundreds of dealer groups for real-time diagnostics, AI-powered autonomous combines and tractors ship in production, and the company targets doubling Precision Tech to 10% of agriculture revenue by 2030 — representing quantifiable AI-related revenue growth [3].

infinite_game.summary: CNH under CEO Gerrit Marx demonstrates genuine infinite game characteristics through its framing of agricultural technology as a generational mission connected to global food security. The company has maintained R&D investment through the worst agricultural equipment downcycle in decades, and the 2022 Iveco spinoff showed willingness to fundamentally restructure. However, the recent downturn management has been cautious — protecting margins and cutting costs rather than making counter-cyclical investments that Sinek would recognize as courageous leadership.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's purpose of 'Breaking New Ground' connects equipment manufacturing to the challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably. CEO Marx has explicitly framed AI as enabling 'tomorrow's agronomist' to help farmers make bigger decisions better [1][3]. This is a genuine and partially lived cause — R&D investment continues through the downcycle, and precision agriculture technology is being deployed to advance it. However, the cause has not yet been tested through decades of consistent execution under CNH's current form (post-2022 spinoff).

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership reorganization under CEO Marx (appointed July 2024) with a lean, agile structure delineating clear accountability [1]. Glassdoor reviews show 3.3/5 culture rating — adequate but with criticisms of siloed departments and unclear accountability [3]. The recent leadership transition adds uncertainty, though the reorganization itself signals intent to improve organizational effectiveness.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar, framing its product roadmap in terms of capability gaps to close. Management language consistently focuses on delivering better products and improving margins rather than 'beating' competitors [1]. The precision agriculture technology roadmap is explicitly designed to close the gap with Deere's technology leadership.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): CNH spun off Iveco Group in 2022 to create a pure-play agriculture/construction company — a meaningful strategic simplification that demonstrates willingness to fundamentally restructure [1]. The shift from pure iron manufacturer to tech-integrated 'precision agriculture platform' provider also represents meaningful strategic evolution, though this is incremental rather than transformative.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in decades with Q1 2026 net income of only $10M, CNH maintained 15+ new tractor launches and 30+ precision tech releases through 2027, continuing to invest in capability [1]. CEO Marx's statement that 'CNH is moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects investment conviction. However, the simultaneous cost-cutting and margin protection measures suggest balancing near-term survival with long-term investment.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality agricultural equipment franchise navigating a cyclical trough with visible recovery on the horizon. Q1 2026 revenue stabilized at $3.83B (flat YoY after -9% in FY2025), and management reaffirmed full-year guidance with agricultural demand recovery expected beginning in 2027 [1]. The forward PEG of 0.6 suggests the market is pricing in meaningful earnings recovery from the current trough. CNH's precision agriculture technology stack — with AI-powered autonomous equipment shipping in production, the AI Tech Assistant deployed across dealer networks, and a target of doubling Precision Tech to 10% of agriculture revenue by 2030 — positions it to capture greater value per unit as the cycle turns [3]. At 14.4x forward earnings, patient investors are buying a global agricultural equipment leader at trough multiples with strong faith alignment (Inspire score 64).
thesis_continued: The near-term bear case remains severe: Q1 2026 net income collapsed to $10M (from $132M adjusted in Q1 2025), profit margin sits at 2.1%, and D/E of 333.7% exceeds IOWN's scoring floor — elevated leverage that limits financial flexibility during the extended downturn [1][4]. Agricultural industry retail demand is forecast down another 5% in 2026 with high-horsepower North American tractors facing 15-20% demand declines. Tariffs created 110bps of EBIT margin headwind with expectations to worsen in 2026. The 5-year revenue CAGR of approximately -8% reflects structural contraction from peak levels, and the dividend has been cut from $0.47/share to $0.10/share, signaling management's own uncertainty about near-term cash generation. Competitors Deere and AGCO are aggressively advancing autonomous and electric equipment that could outpace CNH's technology roadmap.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and farmer income stabilizes, creating significant operating leverage on CNH's fixed-cost manufacturing base

Precision agriculture technology monetization — CNH's AI-powered autonomous systems, AI Tech Assistant, and crop management platform driving software/service revenue at margins above traditional equipment

Forward PEG of 0.6 implies the market undervalues the earnings recovery trajectory — any confirmation of 2027 demand inflection could drive significant multiple expansion from the current 14.4x forward P/E
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2026 as commodity prices fail to recover, delaying farmer capital expenditure and creating further earnings pressure on a company already at $10M quarterly net income

D/E of 333.7% exceeds IOWN's scoring floor — elevated leverage from the financial services arm limits flexibility and creates downside risk if credit conditions tighten or equipment residual values decline

John Deere accelerating autonomous/electric equipment rollouts and precision agriculture software monetization, positioning Deere ahead of CNH in the technology leadership race that will define next-cycle competitive dynamics
